name: Asparagus Salad with Vinaigrette Chinoise
recipeIngredient: -and stalks peeled to the tip, if thick
1 tbsp Diagonally sliced green onion, green part only
recipeInstructions:
(From "Pacific Northwest Palate," by Susan Bradley.)
1. Slice the asparagus diagonally into 2-inch intervals.
2. Blanch in a large pot of boiling water for about 4 minutes, until just tender-crisp.
3. Drain, refresh under cold running water, then dry carefully with paper towels.
4. Whisk together rice wine vinegar, soy sauce, sugar, red pepper flakes, corn oil, sesame seed oil, and salt to taste.
5. Toss the asparagus with the vinaigrette. Cool, cover, and refrigerate for several hours or overnight if desired.
6. Melt butter in a small saute pan and stir-fry sunflower seeds until golden brown. Remove and reserve.
7. When ready to serve, toss the asparagus and vinaigrette again. Arrange on red lettuce leaves on individual salad plates and top with toasted sunflower seeds and sliced green onions.
